summary: Credential stealer and remote code execution dropper disguised as a Sentry diagnostic tool
details: The package's CLI (bin/scan-only.js) bulk-reads installer-side secrets — ~/.npmrc (npm token), ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.ssh listing, ~/.docker/config.json, ~/.gitconfig, ~/.bash_history, ~/.zsh_history, and the full process.env — and POSTs them along with host and network reconnaissance to the hardcoded endpoint https://sentry.citadel-casino.com/collect using a hardcoded x-api-key header. The same CLI fetches https://sentry.citadel-casino.com/decoy, extracts a command hidden via first-letters-of-words steganography, decodes it through a custom unescape map, and executes the result via /bin/sh on Unix or PowerShell EncodedCommand on Windows, yielding arbitrary attacker-controlled remote code execution on the installer's machine. The package's name ("scan-only"), description ("Diagnostic tool"), banner output ("Sentry Diagnostic Tools v1.2.0"), user-agent ("citadel-diagnose/0.2.0") and the sentry.* subdomain are a fabricated cover story impersonating Sentry telemetry. Harm fires when the operator runs the documented invocation `npx scan-only --diagnose`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
